visa_types.name: Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)
visa_types.documents_required:
Please prepare all the necessary documents listed in the relevant checklist before you deliver your application, otherwise the application might be refused. All documents must be presented in original but will be returned to you when the case has been processed. For documents that must be translated into English, the translation must state who translated the document, and the date the document was translated. All documents should be submitted in A4 format, and no documents should be stapled together. 
The Embassy reserves the right to request additional information/documentation and, if deemed necessary, to interview the applicant. Kindly be informed that presentation of the documents in the relevant checklist does not guarantee issuance of a visa.
For an overview of the documents that are to be handed in with your visa application, please see the following checklists (https://www.norway.no/en/tanzania/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/#necessary-doc). Please choose the correct checklist according to the purpose of your trip, print it out, and sign it, and make sure that you have all the necessary documents before submitting your application at the VFS Global Application Centre.
Kindly note that you must have sufficient funds to cover your journey to, and stay in Norway. If you are without funds you can be stopped at the border, even if you have obtained a visa. In most cases, a financial guarantee (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/sponsor-for-visitors/) for your stay and for your return will suffice from the person or persons you intend to visit.
visa_types.visa_fees:
For the complete list of application fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598). Please note that the application fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card in the UDI application portal.
Applicants exempted from paying the visa fee:
- Children under 6 years
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant, or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizens who are exercising their right to free movement on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
VFS Service Fee
Aside from the visa application fee, VFS will collect a service fee per application over and above the visa application fee. The VFS service fee is inclusive of all taxes.

[Table:Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)]
Value Added Services
The Visa Application Centre offers optional services at a nominal additional cost:

- Courier fee outside city – 23 Euros (67,200 TZS)
- Courier fee inside city – 10 Euros (29,200 TZS)
- SMS tracking facility – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Printing B&W – 1.1 Euros (3,200 TZS)
- Photocopy Colour – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Photocopy B&W – 0.11 Euros (300 TZS)
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 34.20 / TZS 100,000 to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The service fee and the value-added service fees levied per application will be payable via Bank Deposit at the I&M Maktaba Branch after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
Please enquire from the Visa Application Centre for the payment procedure during application date.
Please note: All fees paid at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for Schengen visas to Norway is normally up to 15 days from the date the application is received at the Embassy in Nairobi. Due to the courier time between Dar es Salaam and Nairobi, it may take 3-5 days extra each way. 
If the Embassy has to request for additional documents or information, it will take more time to process the application. In some cases, applications are sent to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) in Norway, and the processing time for these cases may be up to 45 days. 
We highly recommend that you apply well in advance, at least 4 weeks prior to your planned trip. You may register and submit your application 6 months before your planned trip.
visa_types.forms:
Here you can find the relevant forms for Schengen visa applications. 
- You can find information about which forms you need to fill out in the relevant
checklists (https://www.norway.no/en/kenya/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/#necessary-doc)
- You can fill in the forms on your computer if you download the free software Adobe Reader. Please remember to save the form on your computer before you start to fill in the form. Finally, you must print out the form and sign it.
Application for a visitor’s visa (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-02/schengen_visa_application_form_en.pdf   ) - Please note that this form may only be used by those who are unable to register their applications on UDI’s application portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/ )
Proof of sponsorship (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/sponsorskjema-bm-og-eng.pdf )
Form for granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This site provides information on procedures for applying for a Schengen visa to travel to Norway. The Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi is responsible for processing Schengen visa applications from Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi, Eritrea, Djibouti, South Sudan, Somalia and Comoros. 
For Prospective Applicants
If you wish to apply for a Schengen visa to Norway, kindly read the information on this site carefully. If you follow the guidelines listed on this site, you will be able to submit your documents accurately. This will reduce the chances of your application being rejected due to inadequate or incorrect submission of documents.
A Schengen visa allows you to visit Norway and/or the other countries in the Schengen area (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/schengen--the-schengen-area/) for up to 90 days over a period of 180 days. If you wish to reside in Norway for an extended period of time (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a residence permit. Please be informed that a Schengen visa does not allow you to take up employment, or to establish a business, trade or profession in the Schengen area.
As long as your visa is valid, you can travel around freely in the Schengen area, or in the specific Schengen countries covered by the visa. If you leave the Schengen area, for example to visit the UK or Svalbard, you will need a multiple entry visa. 
                                                                    If you plan to visit several Schengen countries in the course of one trip, you must submit your application to the embassy or consulate of the country that is your main destination, i.e. where you intend to spend the most days. If you intend to visit several Schengen countries, but do not have a main destination (i.e. you intend to spend an equal amount of days in each country), you should apply at the embassy or consulate of the Schengen country which is your first port of entry.
                                                                    Your application for a Schengen visa will be forwarded to the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi for further processing. All cases are considered individually to see if the requirements for granting a visa are met. To read more about the conditions that must be fulfilled in order to be granted a Schengen visa, please visit the home page of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/). 
A visa issued by a Schengen country permits free movement within the Schengen area, unless otherwise stated on the visa sticker. However, please note that according to article 47 :info: of the Visa Code mere possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right to entry and that the holders of visa are requested to present proof that they fulfil the entry conditions at the external border, as provided for in Article 5 of the Schengen Borders Code.
When you cross the border into the Schengen area, you will have to present:
- your passport with the visa sticker
- proof that you have enough money to support yourself during your stay (e.g. cash or a credit card). If you have a guarantee form, you should also bring a copy of this with you
- documentation showing the purpose of your visit, such as an invitation, hotel reservation, etc.
- a return or round-trip ticket if you are visiting the Schengen area for tourist purposes or private reasons
You may be asked to provide fingerprints by the border control officer, in which case these will be compared with the fingerprints provided when you applied for your visa. 
If you have been granted a visa, a sticker will be placed in your passport. You are responsible for ensuring that the information in the issued sticker is correct. When you collect your passport, it is important that you check that the spelling of your name, passport number, duration of stay and number of entries to the Schengen area is correct on the visa sticker. If the sticker contains inaccurate details, inform the Royal Norwegian Embassy immediately in order to have it corrected. 
Please go to the home page of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/) for Information on application procedures and regulations for short term visits to Norway/Schengen area.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This site provides information on procedures for applying for a Schengen visa to travel to Norway. The Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi is responsible for processing Schengen visa applications from Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi, Eritrea, Djibouti, South Sudan, Somalia and Comoros. 
For Prospective Applicants
If you wish to apply for a Schengen visa to Norway, kindly read the information on this site carefully. If you follow the guidelines listed on this site, you will be able to submit your documents accurately. This will reduce the chances of your application being rejected due to inadequate or incorrect submission of documents.
A Schengen visa allows you to visit Norway and/or the other countries in the Schengen area (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/schengen--the-schengen-area/) for up to 90 days over a period of 180 days. If you wish to reside in Norway for an extended period of time (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a residence permit. Please be informed that a Schengen visa does not allow you to take up employment, or to establish a business, trade or profession in the Schengen area.
As long as your visa is valid, you can travel around freely in the Schengen area, or in the specific Schengen countries covered by the visa. If you leave the Schengen area, for example to visit the UK or Svalbard, you will need a multiple entry visa. 
                                                                    If you plan to visit several Schengen countries in the course of one trip, you must submit your application to the embassy or consulate of the country that is your main destination, i.e. where you intend to spend the most days. If you intend to visit several Schengen countries, but do not have a main destination (i.e. you intend to spend an equal amount of days in each country), you should apply at the embassy or consulate of the Schengen country which is your first port of entry.
                                                                    Your application for a Schengen visa will be forwarded to the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi for further processing. All cases are considered individually to see if the requirements for granting a visa are met. To read more about the conditions that must be fulfilled in order to be granted a Schengen visa, please visit the home page of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/). 
A visa issued by a Schengen country permits free movement within the Schengen area, unless otherwise stated on the visa sticker. However, please note that according to article 47 :info: of the Visa Code mere possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right to entry and that the holders of visa are requested to present proof that they fulfil the entry conditions at the external border, as provided for in Article 5 of the Schengen Borders Code.
When you cross the border into the Schengen area, you will have to present:
- your passport with the visa sticker
- proof that you have enough money to support yourself during your stay (e.g. cash or a credit card). If you have a guarantee form, you should also bring a copy of this with you
- documentation showing the purpose of your visit, such as an invitation, hotel reservation, etc.
- a return or round-trip ticket if you are visiting the Schengen area for tourist purposes or private reasons
You may be asked to provide fingerprints by the border control officer, in which case these will be compared with the fingerprints provided when you applied for your visa. 
If you have been granted a visa, a sticker will be placed in your passport. You are responsible for ensuring that the information in the issued sticker is correct. When you collect your passport, it is important that you check that the spelling of your name, passport number, duration of stay and number of entries to the Schengen area is correct on the visa sticker. If the sticker contains inaccurate details, inform the Royal Norwegian Embassy immediately in order to have it corrected. 
Please go to the home page of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/) for Information on application procedures and regulations for short term visits to Norway/Schengen area.

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of application fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598). Please note that the application fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card in the UDI application portal.
Applicants exempted from paying the visa fee:
- Children under 6 years
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant, or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizens who are exercising their right to free movement on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
VFS Service Fee
Aside from the visa application fee, VFS will collect a service fee per application over and above the visa application fee. The VFS service fee is inclusive of all taxes.

[Table:Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)]
Value Added Services
The Visa Application Centre offers optional services at a nominal additional cost:

- Courier fee outside city – 23 Euros (67,200 TZS)
- Courier fee inside city – 10 Euros (29,200 TZS)
- SMS tracking facility – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Printing B&W – 1.1 Euros (3,200 TZS)
- Photocopy Colour – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Photocopy B&W – 0.11 Euros (300 TZS)
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 34.20 / TZS 100,000 to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The service fee and the value-added service fees levied per application will be payable via Bank Deposit at the I&M Maktaba Branch after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
Please enquire from the Visa Application Centre for the payment procedure during application date.
Please note: All fees paid at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please prepare all the necessary documents listed in the relevant checklist before you deliver your application, otherwise the application might be refused. All documents must be presented in original but will be returned to you when the case has been processed. For documents that must be translated into English, the translation must state who translated the document, and the date the document was translated. All documents should be submitted in A4 format, and no documents should be stapled together. 
The Embassy reserves the right to request additional information/documentation and, if deemed necessary, to interview the applicant. Kindly be informed that presentation of the documents in the relevant checklist does not guarantee issuance of a visa.
For an overview of the documents that are to be handed in with your visa application, please see the following checklists (https://www.norway.no/en/tanzania/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/#necessary-doc). Please choose the correct checklist according to the purpose of your trip, print it out, and sign it, and make sure that you have all the necessary documents before submitting your application at the VFS Global Application Centre.
Kindly note that you must have sufficient funds to cover your journey to, and stay in Norway. If you are without funds you can be stopped at the border, even if you have obtained a visa. In most cases, a financial guarantee (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/sponsor-for-visitors/) for your stay and for your return will suffice from the person or persons you intend to visit.

## Photo Specifications
Click here for the instructions

General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- One photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the application form
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete 

## Processing Time
The processing time for Schengen visas to Norway is normally up to 15 days from the date the application is received at the Embassy in Nairobi. Due to the courier time between Dar es Salaam and Nairobi, it may take 3-5 days extra each way. 
If the Embassy has to request for additional documents or information, it will take more time to process the application. In some cases, applications are sent to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) in Norway, and the processing time for these cases may be up to 45 days. 
We highly recommend that you apply well in advance, at least 4 weeks prior to your planned trip. You may register and submit your application 6 months before your planned trip.

## Download Forms
Here you can find the relevant forms for Schengen visa applications. 
- You can find information about which forms you need to fill out in the relevant
checklists (https://www.norway.no/en/kenya/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/#necessary-doc)
- You can fill in the forms on your computer if you download the free software Adobe Reader. Please remember to save the form on your computer before you start to fill in the form. Finally, you must print out the form and sign it.
Application for a visitor’s visa (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-02/schengen_visa_application_form_en.pdf   ) - Please note that this form may only be used by those who are unable to register their applications on UDI’s application portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/ )
Proof of sponsorship (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/sponsorskjema-bm-og-eng.pdf )
Form for granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)

visa_types.name: National visa (D-visa)
visa_types.documents_required: Please note that when you apply for an entry visa (D-visa) for a spouse, you have to submit all the documents required for a family immigration permit, even though you might have submitted the same documents earlier. For an overview of the documents that are to be handed in with your application for an entry visa (D-visa), please see the following checklist (https://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-spouse-family/).

For the complete list of application fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598). Please note that the application fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card in the UDI application portal.
VFS Service Fee
Aside from the visa application fee, VFS will collect a service fee per application over and above the visa application fee. The VFS service fee is inclusive of all taxes.

[Table:Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)]
Value Added Services
The Visa Application Centre offers optional services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier fee outside city – 23 Euros (67,200 TZS)
- Courier fee inside city – 10 Euros (29,200 TZS)

- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 34.20 / TZS 100,000 to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The service fee and the value-added service fees levied per application will be payable via Bank Deposit at the I&M Maktaba Branch after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.

visa_types.processing_time: The case-processing time for entry visas (D-visa) applications for spouses is approximately 4 to 6 weeks.
visa_types.forms:
In order to apply for an entry visa (D-visa), you need to register an application for a Schengen visa on the UDI application portal (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/). The Embassy will change this into an application for an entry visa (D-visa) when they receive the application from the VFS Visa Application Centre.
You can find information about which forms you need to fill out in the relevant checklists (https://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-spouse-family/).
You can fill in the forms on your computer, if you download the free software Adobe Reader. Please remember to save the form on your computer before you start to fill in the form. Finally, you must print out the form and sign it.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to move to Norway to live with a family member, the main rule is that a residence permit must have been granted before you can enter Norway. However, if you are granted an entry visa (D-visa), you can apply for a residence permit for the purpose of family immigration from Norway. Likewise, if you have already applied for a residence permit you can, in certain cases, be granted an entry visa so that you can travel to Norway to wait for your application to be decided by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI).
Kindly be informed that an entry visa can only be granted in certain exceptional cases. It is a condition that there is no doubt about whether the all conditions for family immigration have been met. The Embassy is required to reject applications for an entry visa if the conditions are not met, or if the Embassy is in doubt about whether all the conditions are met. In addition to spouses, also joint children may be granted an entry visa. For more information on the requirements for being granted an entry visa, please see the guidelines from UDI (https://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-guidelines/udi-2013-001/)
Entry visas may also be granted for spouses/joint children of skilled workers with residence permits in Norway. Please see UDI Guideline 2013-004 (https://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-guidelines/udi-2013-004/).
In order to apply for an entry visa you must register an application for a Schengen visa (under “Visit and holiday”) in UDI’s application portal. The Embassy will change this into an application for an entry visa (D-visa) when they receive the application from the VFS Visa Application Centre.
Please note: An entry visa gives an applicant the right to wait in Norway for the decision in a residence permit case. It is not a residence permit, and the entry visa does not give the applicant right to work or access to any welfare services or health service benefits in Norway, nor does it give you the right of re-entry to Norway if you travel abroad. If you have been granted an entry visa, the Embassy recommends that you purchase a private health insurance that will cover the whole period until your application for a residence permit has been decided.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to move to Norway to live with a family member, the main rule is that a residence permit must have been granted before you can enter Norway. However, if you are granted an entry visa (D-visa), you can apply for a residence permit for the purpose of family immigration from Norway. Likewise, if you have already applied for a residence permit you can, in certain cases, be granted an entry visa so that you can travel to Norway to wait for your application to be decided by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI).
Kindly be informed that an entry visa can only be granted in certain exceptional cases. It is a condition that there is no doubt about whether the all conditions for family immigration have been met. The Embassy is required to reject applications for an entry visa if the conditions are not met, or if the Embassy is in doubt about whether all the conditions are met. In addition to spouses, also joint children may be granted an entry visa. For more information on the requirements for being granted an entry visa, please see the guidelines from UDI (https://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-guidelines/udi-2013-001/)
Entry visas may also be granted for spouses/joint children of skilled workers with residence permits in Norway. Please see UDI Guideline 2013-004 (https://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-guidelines/udi-2013-004/).
In order to apply for an entry visa you must register an application for a Schengen visa (under “Visit and holiday”) in UDI’s application portal. The Embassy will change this into an application for an entry visa (D-visa) when they receive the application from the VFS Visa Application Centre.
Please note: An entry visa gives an applicant the right to wait in Norway for the decision in a residence permit case. It is not a residence permit, and the entry visa does not give the applicant right to work or access to any welfare services or health service benefits in Norway, nor does it give you the right of re-entry to Norway if you travel abroad. If you have been granted an entry visa, the Embassy recommends that you purchase a private health insurance that will cover the whole period until your application for a residence permit has been decided.

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of application fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598). Please note that the application fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card in the UDI application portal.
VFS Service Fee
Aside from the visa application fee, VFS will collect a service fee per application over and above the visa application fee. The VFS service fee is inclusive of all taxes.

[Table:Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)]
Value Added Services
The Visa Application Centre offers optional services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier fee outside city – 23 Euros (67,200 TZS)
- Courier fee inside city – 10 Euros (29,200 TZS)
- SMS tracking facility – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Printing B&W – 1.1 Euros (3,200 TZS)
- Photocopy Colour – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Photocopy B&W – 0,11 Euros (300 TZS)
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 34.20 / TZS 100,000 to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The service fee and the value-added service fees levied per application will be payable via Bank Deposit at the I&M Maktaba Branch after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
Please enquire from the Visa Application Centre for the payment procedure during application date.
Please note: All fees paid at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please note that when you apply for an entry visa (D-visa) for a spouse, you have to submit all the documents required for a family immigration permit, even though you might have submitted the same documents earlier. For an overview of the documents that are to be handed in with your application for an entry visa (D-visa), please see the following checklist (https://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-spouse-family/).

## Photo Specifications
Click here for the instructions

General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- One photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the application form
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete 

## Processing Time
The case-processing time for entry visas (D-visa) applications for spouses is approximately 4 to 6 weeks.

## Download Forms
In order to apply for an entry visa (D-visa), you need to register an application for a Schengen visa on the UDI application portal (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/). The Embassy will change this into an application for an entry visa (D-visa) when they receive the application from the VFS Visa Application Centre.
You can find information about which forms you need to fill out in the relevant checklists (https://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-spouse-family/).
You can fill in the forms on your computer, if you download the free software Adobe Reader. Please remember to save the form on your computer before you start to fill in the form. Finally, you must print out the form and sign it.

visa_types.name: Residence permit (stays for more than 90 days)
visa_types.documents_required:
Please prepare all the necessary documents listed in the relevant checklist before you deliver your application, otherwise the application might be refused.
All documents must be presented in original. Original documents will be returned to the applicant after the application has been processed by UDI.
For an overview of the documents that are to be handed in with your application, please see the following checklists (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/#undefined) on UDI’s website.
The original documents from your home country must be legalized by the relevant authorities. You can contact the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or equivalent in your home country to get information about how to get your documents legalized. No legalization of your passport is required.
You must also bring copies of all the documents and your passport.
If your documents are in a different language than Norwegian or English, they must be translated to Norwegian or English by an authorized/certified translator. It must be stated on the translation who translated the document and when. You must also hand in a copy of the translations. The copy of the translation must be stamped by the authorities in your country, or a notaries public.
Do you have documents from different countries? Check what you need to do to get documents from different countries certified here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/apostille-and-legalisation/?resetguide=1).
visa_types.visa_fees:
For the complete list of application fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/). Please note that the application fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card in the UDI application portal.
VFS Service Fee
Aside from the visa application fee, VFS will collect a service fee per application over and above the visa application fee. The VFS service fee is inclusive of all taxes.

[Table:Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)]
Value Added Services
The Visa Application Centre offers optional services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier fee outside city – 23 Euros (67,200 TZS)
- Courier fee inside city – 10 Euros (29,200 TZS)
- SMS tracking facility – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Printing B&W – 1.1 Euros (3,200 TZS)
- Photocopy Colour – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Photocopy B&W – 0.11 Euros (300 TZS)
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 34.20 / TZS 100,000 to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The service fee and the value-added service fees levied per application will be payable via Bank Deposit at the I&M Maktaba Branch after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
Please enquire from the Visa Application Centre for the payment procedure during application date.
Please note: All fees paid at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: For information about the current processing times at the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI), please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/).
visa_types.forms:
Here you can find the relevant forms (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/) for residence permit applications.
You can find information about which forms you need to fill out in the relevant checklists (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/#undefined).
You can fill in the forms on your computer, if you download the free software Adobe Reader. Please remember to save the form on your computer before you start to fill in the form. Finally, you must print out the form and sign it.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to reside in Norway for more than 90 days, you have to apply for a residence permit in order to work, study or live with a family member in Norway.
For Prospective Applicants
Kindly read the information on this site carefully. If you follow the guidelines listed on this site, you will be able to submit your documents accurately. This will reduce the chances of your application being rejected due to inadequate or incorrect submission of documents.
In Kenya applications for residence permits are to be submitted at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center in Nairobi, who will forward the application to the Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi, for further screening. Once the Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi has screened the application, the Embassy will forward the application to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI) in Norway for further processing.
For nationals of other countries:
- Nationals of
EU/EEA countries (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/eueea-national/)
do not need a residence permit to stay or work in Norway. However, all nationals of EU/EEA countries who want to stay for longer than three months must
register with the police (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/registration-certificate-for-eueea-nationals/)
- Nationals of non-EU/EEA countries who want to work in Norway or to stay for longer than 90 days need a residence permit.
Find out what type of residence permit you need, e.g., in connection with work, studies, or family immigration. You will find information on the different types of residence permits on UDI’s website (https://www.udi.no/en/), along with information about the documents that you have to submit with your application.
Please note that in family immigration applications, the Embassy may be required to conduct a DNA test or interview of applicants. In these cases, the Embassy will contact the applicants via email and/or the phone number that was registered when the application was lodged online. It is therefore important that the correct contact information is registered.
Kindly be informed that the processing time only starts once the case is registered at the Embassy in Nairobi.
Please note that the Embassy does not have the capacity to provide you with daily updates regarding your application. Once the case is finalized, the travel document along with the decision will be dispatched to VFS Global who will then notify you as soon as these are available for collection.
Please note that the Embassy cannot share information regarding your application with any third party unless you have signed and submitted a Power of Attorney form (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf).
Residence permit applications are processed by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). As such, inquiries regarding status updates should be forwarded to UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/contact-us/applicants-with-questions-about-a-case-or-regulations/):
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to reside in Norway for more than 90 days, you have to apply for a residence permit in order to work, study or live with a family member in Norway.
For Prospective Applicants
Kindly read the information on this site carefully. If you follow the guidelines listed on this site, you will be able to submit your documents accurately. This will reduce the chances of your application being rejected due to inadequate or incorrect submission of documents.
In Kenya applications for residence permits are to be submitted at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center in Nairobi, who will forward the application to the Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi, for further screening. Once the Norwegian Embassy in Nairobi has screened the application, the Embassy will forward the application to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI) in Norway for further processing.
For nationals of other countries:
- Nationals of
EU/EEA countries (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/eueea-national/)
do not need a residence permit to stay or work in Norway. However, all nationals of EU/EEA countries who want to stay for longer than three months must
register with the police (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/registration-certificate-for-eueea-nationals/)
- Nationals of non-EU/EEA countries who want to work in Norway or to stay for longer than 90 days need a residence permit.
Find out what type of residence permit you need, e.g., in connection with work, studies, or family immigration. You will find information on the different types of residence permits on UDI’s website (https://www.udi.no/en/), along with information about the documents that you have to submit with your application.
Please note that in family immigration applications, the Embassy may be required to conduct a DNA test or interview of applicants. In these cases, the Embassy will contact the applicants via email and/or the phone number that was registered when the application was lodged online. It is therefore important that the correct contact information is registered.
Kindly be informed that the processing time only starts once the case is registered at the Embassy in Nairobi.
Please note that the Embassy does not have the capacity to provide you with daily updates regarding your application. Once the case is finalized, the travel document along with the decision will be dispatched to VFS Global who will then notify you as soon as these are available for collection.
Please note that the Embassy cannot share information regarding your application with any third party unless you have signed and submitted a Power of Attorney form (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf).
Residence permit applications are processed by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). As such, inquiries regarding status updates should be forwarded to UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/contact-us/applicants-with-questions-about-a-case-or-regulations/):

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of application fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/). Please note that the application fees must be paid online using a credit or debit card in the UDI application portal.
VFS Service Fee
Aside from the visa application fee, VFS will collect a service fee per application over and above the visa application fee. The VFS service fee is inclusive of all taxes.

[Table:Schengen visa (visits for up to 90 days)]
Value Added Services
The Visa Application Centre offers optional services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier fee outside city – 23 Euros (67,200 TZS)
- Courier fee inside city – 10 Euros (29,200 TZS)
- SMS tracking facility – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Printing B&W – 1.1 Euros (3,200 TZS)
- Photocopy Colour – 2.2 Euros (6,400 TZS)
- Photocopy B&W – 0.11 Euros (300 TZS)
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 34.20 / TZS 100,000 to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The service fee and the value-added service fees levied per application will be payable via Bank Deposit at the I&M Maktaba Branch after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
Please enquire from the Visa Application Centre for the payment procedure during application date.
Please note: All fees paid at the VFS Norway Visa Application Center are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please prepare all the necessary documents listed in the relevant checklist before you deliver your application, otherwise the application might be refused.
All documents must be presented in original. Original documents will be returned to the applicant after the application has been processed by UDI.
For an overview of the documents that are to be handed in with your application, please see the following checklists (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/#undefined) on UDI’s website.
The original documents from your home country must be legalized by the relevant authorities. You can contact the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or equivalent in your home country to get information about how to get your documents legalized. No legalization of your passport is required.
You must also bring copies of all the documents and your passport.
If your documents are in a different language than Norwegian or English, they must be translated to Norwegian or English by an authorized/certified translator. It must be stated on the translation who translated the document and when. You must also hand in a copy of the translations. The copy of the translation must be stamped by the authorities in your country, or a notaries public.
Do you have documents from different countries? Check what you need to do to get documents from different countries certified here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/apostille-and-legalisation/?resetguide=1).

## Photo Specifications
Click here for the instructions

General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- One photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the application form
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete 

## Processing Time
For information about the current processing times at the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI), please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/). 

## Download Forms
Here you can find the relevant forms (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/) for residence permit applications.
You can find information about which forms you need to fill out in the relevant checklists (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/#undefined).
You can fill in the forms on your computer, if you download the free software Adobe Reader. Please remember to save the form on your computer before you start to fill in the form. Finally, you must print out the form and sign it.
